message:
feat(raptorq): add systematic conformance replay entries and rustfmt cleanup

Expand the D9 replay catalog with replay entries for the systematic
conformance and property-based test vectors (known_vector_small_block,
repair_symbol_matches_rfc_equation_projection, and others), linking each
to its unit test and E2E conformance script. These entries close the
coverage gap for systematic-family tests that previously had no replay
catalog linkage.

The unit test matrix documentation is updated to reflect that systematic
conformance/property vectors now emit schema-aligned context and resolve
to D9 replay IDs, with only drop/fuzz families remaining partially
unstructured. Gap descriptions narrowed to reflect the shrinking scope.

Also applies rustfmt formatting to several assertion macros in
src/raptorq/tests.rs that were previously on single long lines —
purely cosmetic, no logic changes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

-- unit-level structured context is present for edge-case paths, builder-path send/receive tests, and systematic/GF256 deterministic regression sentinels; full suite-wide replay-id propagation remains **partial** pending D7 coverage completion
+- unit-level structured context is present for edge-case paths, builder-path send/receive tests, systematic conformance/property vectors, and systematic/GF256 deterministic regression sentinels; full suite-wide replay-id propagation remains **partial** pending D7 coverage completion
 
 ## Replay Catalog (D9)
 
@@ -107,8 +107,8 @@ Profile tags represented in catalog entries:
 Open gaps identified during matrix pass:
 
 1. Unit suites do not yet consistently emit explicit `replay_ref` IDs on every failure path.
-2. Some non-builder GF256/property-style tests still rely on plain assertion text without schema-aligned key/value context.
-3. Matrix row status is still `partial` for systematic/GF256 families until schema-aligned failure context is universal.
+2. Some GF256, fuzz, and high-loss/drop property paths still rely on plain assertion text without schema-aligned key/value context.
+3. Matrix row status is still `partial` for systematic/GF256 families until those remaining paths emit schema-aligned failure context universally.
